title: carex collinsii
intro: Carex collinsii, also known as Collins' sedge, is a type of plant species belonging to the family Cyperaceae, commonly found in wetland areas. Traditionally, Carex collinsii has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating urinary issues due to its diuretic and antiseptic qualities. The plant's health benefits include its ability to alleviate kidney stones, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Carex collinsii can be used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
